narrative:
Expect plenty of sunshine for your Tuesday with high temperatures surpassing daily records. There will be a repeat performance on Wednesday, with temperatures a degree or two higher. Meanwhile, low temperatures tonight will be warmer than the normal high temperature for this time in March. The weather through Wednesday daytime will be dry with the following caveats: There could be an isolated afternoon shower or thundershower today, and perhaps a stray shower first thing Wednesday. Then, line of showers and possible thundershowers moves through after midnight Wednesday night, perhaps ending briefly as wet snow Thursday morning, especially across higher elevations.

It's the March Madness edition of the Monday Message, available now for subscribers!

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Into Record Territory

Last Updated Tue March 10 at 9:45AM by Scott Stephens

Expect plenty of sunshine for your Tuesday with high temperatures surpassing daily records. There will be a repeat performance on Wednesday, with temperatures a degree or two higher. Meanwhile, low temperatures tonight will be warmer than the normal high temperature for this time in March. The weather through Wednesday daytime will be dry with the following caveats: There could be an isolated afternoon shower or thundershower today, and perhaps a stray shower first thing Wednesday. Then, line of showers and possible thundershowers moves through after midnight Wednesday night, perhaps ending briefly as wet snow Thursday morning, especially across higher elevations.

It's the March Madness edition of the Monday Message, available now for subscribers!

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

March Madness accurately describes the weather pattern over the coming week with lots of ups-and-downs. The next two days will be characterized by an unseasonable/record-setting air mass with highs likely breaking records for their respective dates. Please see the Almanac link at the top of the page for information on record temperature information.

It’s warm and unstable enough for an isolated afternoon shower or thundershower. Aside from some morning cloudiness and perhaps a passing early shower on Wednesday, expect warm, dry and partly sunny conditions.

A strong cold front arrives late Wednesday night/early Thursday morning. A line of showers and possible thundershowers will roll out of Tennessee and into western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after midnight Wednesday night. Rain ends Thursday morning, perhaps changing to wet snow before ending, especially at higher elevations along the western slopes of the mountains. There could be some dusting-level accumulations in these areas. It will be windy and colder, but really just a notch or two below normal.

Friday will be sunny and seasonable. Saturday looks like the pick of the weekend with sunshine and warmer temperatures as a silent cold front slips past. The front returns northward as a warm front on Sunday with a couple showers, then a strong cold front blows through here on Monday with rain showers changing to snow showers and sharply colder weather for Monday afternoon and Monday night.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 9, 2026

It's March Madness, weather-style, around western North Carolina. We discuss the ups-and-downs of March weather in this week's edition.

Mar 09, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.

Mar 05, 2026
Monday Message for March 2, 2026

It's the first Monday Message for March! We discuss the chilly change for Monday and Tuesday, but the big weather story will be the spring fever that will be breaking out for the second half of the week.
